strands.title: Project Ecosystem Architecture
strands.content: Three-tier research program: (1) Pichay—L1 cache paging for context window, achieved 93% reduction with 0.025% fault rate, validates OS memory analogy under production load; (2) Hamutay—explores transformer as bounded processing unit over explicit external state rather than text predictor, testing H0/H1/H2 hypothesis ladder; (3) Yanantin—L4 hierarchical layer (full memory hierarchy integration). Success of Pichay validates the context-as-cache model and opens the architectural reframe.

key_claims.claim: Context window behaves like L1 cache and can be paged
Transformer should be architected as processing unit over explicit external state, not as text predictor
Pichay paging mechanism achieved 93% reduction with 0.025% fault rate under production load

strands.title: Hamutay Development Status and Blockers
strands.content: Tony is now at the point of formulating foundational questions for Hamutay after earlier exploration with Codex on H0 (bounded processor hypothesis). Codex's work on H0 went off the rails—likely in schema/evaluation harness direction—due to Tony not asking the right framing questions early. Tony recognizes this as his responsibility and is now seeking to ask the 'dumb questions' that should have preceded detailed schema work.

key_claims.claim: H0 exploration with Codex became misdirected before core conceptual questions were settled
The blocker is question formulation, not technical execution

strands.title: Persistent Memory Infrastructure
strands.content: Memory structure at /home/tony/.claude/projects/-home-tony-projects-hamutay/memory/ with foundation files: user_tony.md (profile), MEMORY.md (index). Tensor projection implementation integrated into cognitive processing pipeline.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Specific details about what Codex's H0 exploration produced (schemas, evaluation harnesses, intermediate findings)
declared_losses.why: Not included in the new content provided. These details would be necessary to diagnose precisely what went wrong.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Concrete formulation of the 'dumb questions' Tony believes should have been asked
declared_losses.why: Not yet articulated in conversation—still in the 'wish I'd asked' phase. Intentional open loop until next turn.

declared_losses.what_was_lost: Details of H1 and H2 specifics beyond 'integrate with Pichay's paging' and 'full hierarchy with Yanantin as L4'
declared_losses.why: Mentioned in context but not elaborated in new content. Stored as minimal token for now.

open_questions: What is the foundational conceptual question that should frame H0 exploration for Hamutay?
What went wrong specifically in Codex's H0 work that led to schema/harness tangents?
How should 'bounded processor over explicit external state' be operationalized as a testable hypothesis?
What are the minimal success criteria for H0 before proceeding to H1 integration with Pichay?
How does the processing-unit framing change the evaluation metrics that matter?
instructions_for_next: Next cycle should: (1) capture and integrate the foundational questions Tony articulates about H0 as core intent, (2) track any diagnostic insights about what misdirected Codex's work (this is meta-pattern about question quality), (3) maintain linkage between Hamutay's hypothesis ladder (H0/H1/H2) and the actual technical experiments being run, (4) prepare to organize these questions into a proper problem specification that can guide next research phase, (5) watch for shift from introspection to action—when Tony moves from 'what should I have asked' to 'here's what I'm testing'.
